name: american-dirt
description: Jeanine Cummins's American Dirt — migration narrative following Lydia and her son Luca as they flee a Mexican cartel massacre and journey north through La Bestia to the US border, exploring survival, motherhood, and the human cost of migration
useCase: Understand the migrant journey through fiction, explore the human dimension of border politics, and engage with the controversy of who tells whose story
knownRisks: This is a novel, not a work of nonfiction or journalism
The book has been widely criticized for cultural appropriation by Latinx critics
The author's perspective is that of an outsider to the migrant experience
